title: Identity Through The Trinity
subtitle: Who is God and How Does He See You?
tagline: God Designed You To Be Just Like Him.
fullTitle: Identity Through The Trinity: Exploring your identity through the Father, the Son and Holy Spirit
description: We all have blind spots. Blind spots distort the way you see God, yourself and your ability to hear God clearly. Your true identity is revealed through the Trinity. Receive God's vision and affirmation over your identity. See yourself and God with fresh eyes.

sessionOverviews.title: The Three Identities
sessionOverviews.synopsis: There are three identity perceptions that will steer your entire life. Every decision you make can be traced back to these three perceptions. Are they rooted in reality or distorted by lies we have believed? Sometimes we need an outside voice to reveal our blind spots.

sessionOverviews.title: Identity Shift
sessionOverviews.synopsis: Let's look at biblical characters and observe how their identity perceptions were challenged by God. How has false humility and small thinking hindered you from our destiny. Do you really believe what the Bible says about who God is and who you are?

sessionOverviews.title: Who is God?
sessionOverviews.synopsis: If we are truly made in God's image then who we are is being revealed to us as we uncover who God truly is. God as the Trinity is often misunderstood but it's not as mysterious as we have been taught. How we relate to each member of the Trinity exposes our personal identity perceptions.

sessionOverviews.title: The God Man
sessionOverviews.synopsis: Jesus is fully God and fully man. Are you kinda like Jesus or just like Jesus? According to the bible, everything Jesus owns is yours as a joint heir to His kingdom. What does this mean and why do we fall short of this reality? There must be some lie we have believed.

sessionOverviews.title: Holy Spirit
sessionOverviews.synopsis: There is a difference between agreeing with the testimony of the Holy Spirit and being filled with Holy Spirit power. One is a matter of salvation and the other is a requirement to a Christ-like ministry. What part does the Holy Spirit play in our lives? The answer may surprise you.

sessionOverviews.title: Identity Check
sessionOverviews.synopsis: How do you test if a vessel has holes in it? You fill it, assess the areas that need repair and patch the broken places. Then you refill it and test it again to make sure the repairs are complete. This process is repeated until wholeness is accomplished. Let's see how far you have come in 6 weeks.

sessions.title: The Three Identities
sessions.synopsis: There are three identity perceptions that will steer your entire life. Every decision you make can be traced back to these three perceptions. Are they rooted in reality or distorted by lies we have believed? Sometimes we need an outside voice to reveal our blind spots.
sessions.verse: Now this is eternal life: that they know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ, whom you have sent.
warmUp: What is the most important thing about you?
What is the number one area of growth you want in your relationship with God?

content.text: Throughout this training you will be challenged to look at God and yourself with a fresh perspective. If we are being honest, regardless of how long we have been in relationship with Jesus, our perspective still needs some realignment.

The Bible says Jesus is the author and the finisher of your faith and He will complete the work He has started in you. If that's true, how important is it that you let go of false perceptions that hold you back? It's hard to have a foundation of truth and then stack lies on top of that truth without the lies blatantly sticking out.
There are three identities that will shape the rest of your life.

Each identity is important though they are built upon the foundation of the first and most important Identity. Let's call it the chief cornerstone and the most important perception you will ever have in your life here on earth.
What comes into our minds when we think about God is the most important thing about us.
content.ref: A.W. Tozer, "The Knowledge of the Holy"

questions: Do you agree with Tozer's statement? Why or why not?

content.text: You may know what the bible says about who God is and you may even memorize those declarations but until your personal perception lines up with who God and the bible say He is, you will only grow so far. When you think about who God is, what comes to mind?

A distorted view of God might appear as controlling, distant, manipulative or upset. A healthy view of God as engaged, present and trustworthy will set a foundation for other vital truths to be placed on. To recognize a counterfeit, do you study the authentic or the fake?
Identity Perception: The perspective of a person towards themselves, others and God. This perception could be true or distorted depending on the condition of the eyes they are observed through.
We are not looking for the christian answer. We are looking for the real answer. This exercise is all about finding out where you are in your perception of God.

questions: Take a moment and answer honestly. When I perceive God, I see...

content.text: The second identity exposes what we see in ourselves. This identity is influenced by Identity #1 because we tend to look a lot like the God we perceive. If I perceive a God who is critical, I will find ways to judge myself and others according to my perception. Maybe legalism is so prevalent in Christianity due to critical people creating a God in the image of themselves and then using the bible to justify what they perceive.

When you look in the mirror, what do you see? Do you see a failure? A reject? Or maybe you think too highly of yourself. Maybe you see someone who is self-sufficient and independent. This perception of self will affect the way we live and relate to others on every level of community.
When you are in a group of people do you ever wonder if anyone likes you? Or maybe you find yourself showing favoritism to those who you feel deserve your attention.

questions: Take a moment and answer honestly. When I perceive myself, I see...

lessons.title: Identity #3: How Does God See Me?

content.text: The first two identities are key factors in our awareness of God and self however the third is where the breakthrough happens. This is the one that speaks to your heart and launches you into destiny. You can have a healthy view of who God is and even have faith to believe it. You can have a personal identity rooted in good values and principles but until this third one is activated you could spend years dormant in your calling.
This identity is the breath of God that raises an army of dry bones for battle and is only available through the Spirit of prophecy who is Christ Himself.
The reason this identity is so powerful is because it is not your own perception. It has nothing to do with what you think but everything to do with what God sees and thinks when he looks at you.

This identity has been poured out on so many names throughout the bible and turned timid, unbelieving men into fathers of faith. Men such as Moses, Gideon, David, Paul and many more. All of them had an encounter with the Spirit of Prophecy that changed the course of their lives.

questions: Take as long as necessary to listen and write down everything you hear. God when You look at me, what do You see?

content.text: If you had challenges hearing God, please continue to the bonus lesson: Hindered Hearing.

content.headline: Want to clear up your reception?
content.title: Hindered Hearing
content.subtitle: 5 Things That Could Hold You Back From Hearing God
content.description: All humans have the need to be heard and understood. This attribute of God was placed in us. If God is the foundation of community then our ability to communicate with God will set the stage for all communication with other relationships. Discover what might be blocking your spiritual reception.

sessions.title: Identity Shift
sessions.synopsis: Let's look at biblical characters and observe how their identity perceptions were challenged by God. How has false humility and small thinking hindered you from our destiny. Do you really believe what the Bible says about who God is and who you are?
sessions.verse: Have I not commanded you? Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid; do not be discouraged, for the Lord your God will be with you wherever you go.
warmUp: Take a moment and look back at session one. What are the differences in the way you and God see you?

content.text: One of my favorite examples of identity shift is when God visits Gideon who is hiding from the Midianites in a wine press trying to thresh enough wheat to feed his family. This guy was doing the best he perceived he could under the circumstances. God meets him there in the wine press and calls him out using all three of the foundational identities.

He says "The Lord is with you, mighty man of valor!" Gideon goes on to proclaim his own perception and remind him that He's got the wrong Gideon. "O my Lord, how can I save Israel? Indeed my clan is the weakest in Manasseh, and I am the least in my father's house." It wasn't that He didn't believe God was mighty. He knew the works of the Lord but his personal perception of self was completely based on earthly standards.

Look at this. God calls out the third identity in Gideon while assuring him of God's identity as faithful. God promises that He will be with him all the way through. Gideon then asks for several signs just to be sure that it is God talking. I mean wow!
Sometimes who Jesus says we are is so far off from our personal perception, we need sign after sign to actually grab hold of it by faith and walk in the word God has spoken.

Even more amazing is that God loves you so much that He is willing to show Himself in your unbelief. He is not afraid of our personal perceived limitations. In fact I believe God uses us in our small thinking to glorify His name. If Gideon was a self made mighty man of valor then everyone would expect him to defeat the Midianites. Even Gideon himself was amazed as the prophecy spoken came to fruition.

questions: Do you need God to confirm his word so you can walk out what He has spoken? Or are you able to move forward without confirmation?

content.text: If Jesus, fully God and fully man, were to answer the three identity questions from week one, how would He answer?

Who is God? _____________
Who Am I? ______________
And when Father God looks at Me He sees _______________
Jesus would answer all three identities the same. Love.

He would see no difference between the three. One God, One Spirit, One Love, One body and we are one with the Son as He is one with the Father. (John 17) If you are like me and desire to be just like Jesus then you should start answering these identities as He would.

Accepting agape or "God Love" as your identity and having it take root is a lifelong process of laying yourself down so Holy Spirit can renew your mind. Answer the three identities as Jesus would and you will be moving in a greater level of power and love just like Jesus. This includes intimacy with the trinity, healings, signs and wonders as well as suffering for the sake of His kingdom as so many have.
He is not only Lord and Savior but also a living example of what our lives can look like fully submitted to Holy Spirit.
...he who believes in Me (Jesus), the works that I do he will do also; and greater works than these he will do…
For to this you were called, since Christ also suffered for you, leaving an example for you to follow in his steps.

questions: Do you believe Jesus when He says greater works will be done by those with faith?
Do you ever find yourself limiting what Jesus can do through you?
What is it that holds you back from walking in the identity of Jesus as your own?
What is false humility?

content.text: Declare this out loud: "I am who Jesus says I am."
As a follower of Jesus we have one job. To say "amen." Amen simply means, I agree.

But as surely as God is faithful, our message to you is not 'Yes' and 'No.' For the Son of God, Jesus Christ, who was preached among you by us—by me and Silas and Timothy—was not 'Yes' and 'No,' but in him it has always been 'Yes.' For no matter how many promises God has made, they are 'Yes' in Christ. And so through him the 'Amen' is spoken by us to the glory of God.
In order to walk like Jesus and participate in the miracle power of Holy Spirit, you must agree with God on two things:

1. Who He says He is (God Dependent)
2. Who He says you are (God Confident)
If you agree with God on these two statements you will move into a life of power and love that naturally flows from who you are.

To be God dependent: I agree with who God says He is.
To walk in true humility: I agree with who God says I am.
A life centered in Christ is first grounded in who God is and secondly in who God says you are.

questions: Using the bible and Holy Spirit, write out a list of "Who I Am" statements.
Using the bible and Holy Spirit, write out a list of "Who He Is" statements.

content.text: Did you create your list of agreements? This is your identity battle plan. When the enemy comes to whisper lies about who God is or who you are, you have a weapon ready—the truth of God's Word.
Your agreements are declarations of truth that combat the lies of the enemy.

We want to help you build your battle plan. We've compiled 50 Agreements You Should Make—25 declarations about who God is and 25 declarations about who you are in Christ. Each one is backed by Scripture.
These are not just nice words to read. They are weapons for spiritual warfare. When you feel insecure, declare who God says you are. When you doubt God's goodness, declare who He says He is.

For the weapons of our warfare are not carnal but mighty in God for pulling down strongholds, casting down arguments and every high thing that exalts itself against the knowledge of God, bringing every thought into captivity to the obedience of Christ.

How to use your agreements:
1. Read them aloud daily
2. Memorize the ones that speak to your struggles
3. Declare them when lies attack
4. Share them with your DNA group

content.title: 50 Agreements You Should Make
content.description: 25 declarations about who God is + 25 declarations about who you are

content.title: Build Your Agreement List
content.instruction: Use the space below to write your personal agreements. These will be saved to your device.

columns.label: God is...
columns.placeholder: He is faithful, He is love, He is my healer...

columns.label: I am...
columns.placeholder: I am beloved, I am chosen, I am forgiven...

questions: Which agreements from your list speak most powerfully to your current struggles?
How will you incorporate these declarations into your daily routine?
Share one "Who God Is" and one "Who I Am" agreement with your group.

sessions.title: Who is God?
sessions.synopsis: If we are truly made in God's image then who we are is being revealed to us as we uncover who God truly is. God as the Trinity is often misunderstood but it's not as mysterious as we have been taught. How we relate to each member of the Trinity exposes our personal identity perceptions.
sessions.verse: See how great a love the Father has given us, that we would be called children of God; and in fact we are. For this reason the world does not know us: because it did not know Him.
warmUp: Really, who is God according to the bible? His nature, character and attributes? What does He like and dislike? Is He angry or in a good mood?

content.text: When we look at the image of God painted by the bible, we see one who lacks nothing, unique in all ways, and the essence of divine glory. The bible says he is unchanging, full of mercy and incomprehensible by the human mind. This section in itself is just an attempt at describing an indescribable God using the resources we have to observe in awe what we ultimately cannot fathom. He just is.

The bible says in John 17:24 that "before the foundation of the world." Father God was loving the Son. And In 1 John 4:16 it says ...God is love, and he who abides in love abides in God, and God in him. God can do nothing else other than love because it's not what He does but who He is. This is the nature of the Father's heart not only towards Jesus but towards us as His most favored creation.
God is love. To say God is love is the same as saying God is community.

By nature of love cannot be singular because It requires the relationship of two or more. This is the greatest argument we have for the Father, Son and Holy Spirit as the triune God we worship. If God is love then God has never known solitude. God has always been in community and has extended his community of love to include you and I.

Some may argue that God is one as the Bible says in Deuteronomy 6:4 "Hear, Israel! The Lord is our God, the Lord is one!" That word one is the Hebrew word echad. It's the same word used when God decrees that man and woman shall become ONE flesh. God is one. The Father, Son and Spirit are each members of one family that has always existed and all things they created reflect their nature.
Perichoresis—The relationship of the three persons of the triune God (Father, Son, and Holy Spirit) to one another. Scholars describe this intimate relationship as 'the divine dance.'

Perichoresis is a Greek word that means to go around. It is derived from the Greek word peri, meaning "around" and chorein which means to move. It is the word we get choreography from. Perichoresis is the divine dance of the three is one. It describes the interchanging affection shared within the triune nature of God. It has always existed and it is the image we were created in. It is the relationship we have been invited into.
The Trinity is proof that God is Love.

This is our starting point with understanding who God is. From this place we see how God can humble himself so low to manifest the ultimate expression of love through Jesus on the cross. This understanding destroys the idea that Father God is some Zeus like character who is far away. And Jesus is His lamb that took the weight of His anger. Then Holy Spirit is a mysterious floating energy that comforts us from Father God's punishment when we sin. We cannot hold this view of God along with the foundation that God is love.

But in all these things we overwhelmingly conquer through Him who loved us. For I am convinced that neither death, nor life, nor angels, nor principalities, nor things present, nor things to come, nor powers, nor height, nor depth, nor any other created thing, will be able to separate us from the love of God, which is in Christ Jesus our Lord.

questions: Think about the "perichoresis" relationship of Father, Son and Spirit. Have you ever experienced a relationship that exemplifies true union and authentic community? Explain your desire for this kind of union.
Is there anything scary about this kind of intimate union or are you comfortable with complete vulnerability? Explain why.

content.text: When Jesus was asked to teach his disciples how to pray, He revolutionized prayer for mankind with two words. "Our Father." Not my father but "our" father. He did not look at his sonship as something to keep to himself. He was well acquainted with his Father's love and knew that there was more than enough to share with everyone else.

Often our view of Father God has been shaped by experiences with earthly father figures. Some of us have experienced abuse and abandonment from fathers who fell far short of revealing the heart of God to us. Or maybe we had great fathers who did their best to love us and still they don't compare to the goodness of Papa, or as Jesus would call him in Aramaic, "Abba".

To know the Father or any father and make a judgment on the quality of them as a person, would require us to look at their kids. During the course of my life, you could look at the way I treat my children or ask them how they feel about me as a father. You could later in life look at the people they have become and much of who they are will be determined by how well I have represented the love of God to them. You can tell a lot about people by those they are responsible for. Who was the chief source of love in their lives?

The Bible says in Hebrews that the "Son" "is the radiance of His Glory and the exact representation of His nature." In John 14 one of Jesus' disciples Philip says "Lord, show us the Father and that will be enough for us." Jesus says "How can you ask 'Show us the Father'? Don't you believe that I am in the Father, and that the Father is in me?" So to say that we know God as Father and not know the Son is a contradiction. In order to know the Father we must know the Son as they are one in likeness and nature.

We get that God is Father but what kind of Father is He? We can look at the most famous bible verse of all time. John 3:16 where It reads God loved the world so much that He loved... I know the verse keeps going but let's stop right there. God loves so much that He gave himself for us as the greatest manifestation of love we will ever witness.

Father God is generous. He's not stingy or withholding love from you because you mess up. No. Even when you are a mess, He loves you and will go to great lengths to help clean you up and restore you to Himself.

Father God is humble. He is kind, faithful, forgiving and ever-present. You and I have to come to the understanding that because we are in Christ, the same things that the Father declares over the son, He declares over us. Papa God is not like our earthly father figures. He will not let us down.
Because you are sons, God has sent forth the Spirit of His Son into our hearts, crying, "Abba! Father!"

questions: Father, are there any lies I believe about who you are and how you look at me?

content.text: Sometimes there are lies we believe that we are unaware of without asking for his help. If we ask He will reveal. Remember, His desire is to expose lies not to bring shame. He wants you completely free from deception.
Step 1 - REVEAL: "Father, are there any lies I believe about who you are and how you look at me?"

God reveals lies we believe so we can renounce them from our lives. Verbally offer Him any lies that come up. They cannot remain in you. Take your time as you renounce each lie that has been revealed.
Step 2 - RENOUNCE: Verbally renounce each lie. "I renounce the lie that..."
Now it's time to REPLACE the lies with truth.
Step 3 - REPLACE: "Father, I give you these lies, what do you give me in return?"

Prayer: Papa, speak the truth to me of who you are and help me to not just hear your words but understand them. Help me to receive this truth and that all lies about who you are would fade away. If I have made a God in my mind that is not real I turn from this thinking and give you access to renew my mind. I know you are good but I need you to show me just how good you really are. You are my Dad and I am your beloved child. Thank you for never leaving me even when my perspective is distorted.

questions: What lies were revealed to you?
What truths did God give you in return?

sessions.title: The God Man
sessions.synopsis: Jesus is fully God and fully man. Are you kinda like Jesus or just like Jesus? According to the bible, everything Jesus owns is yours as a joint heir to His kingdom. What does this mean and why do we fall short of this reality? There must be some lie we have believed.
sessions.verse: He is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn over all creation. For by Him all things were created that are in heaven and that are on earth, visible and invisible, whether thrones or dominions or principalities or powers. All things were created through Him and for Him.
warmUp: Christ is the Latin word for Messiah. Messiah simply means anointed one. To be a Christian means to be a little Christ. A little anointed one. Jesus is The Christ and he has anointed us to be like him in every way. What attribute of Jesus do you desire the most? Why?

content.text: If you want to know what the Father is like, look at the Son as the visible aid, the centerpiece and creator of all things, above and below, seen and unseen. The Jews wanted to kill Jesus in John chapter 5 because He claimed that God was His Father, making Himself equal with God. This is one of many occasions where Jesus made proclamations of His own divinity.

In Matthew 9 Jesus heals a crippled man laying on a cot by saying "Take courage, son! Your sins are forgiven." For this, He is accused of blasphemy by the Jewish teachers, as only God has authority to forgive sin. Jesus is making a statement to the culture of pride around Him that refuses to receive Him as the Son of Man. He uses this title "Son of Man" specifically in this scripture as they are all aware this term is used to prophetically describe the Messiah in Daniel 7.
Then Jesus answered and said to them, "Most assuredly, I say to you, the Son can do nothing of Himself, but what He sees the Father do; for whatever He does, the Son also does in like manner."
Hypostatic Union: Jesus Christ being both fully God and fully man. He is both perfectly divine and perfectly human, having two complete and distinct natures at once.

Jesus is God. He is not just The Son of God. He is God in the flesh. He is the God man. This truth that Jesus is fully God and fully man is called the Hypostatic Union. This is the union of divinity and man into one being. Jesus wants us to experience him as God but he uses his humanity as the doorway that opens us up to a whole world of divine encounter.

Jesus walked the earth for 33 years. The first 30 years of his life on earth he walked around as a mere human being without any of the power that comes with being God. The Bible actually says that he emptied himself of the advantages of being God. Every miracle Jesus performed was through the power of the Holy Spirit. This is what makes it possible for us to do the things Jesus does. We all have equal access to the same Spirit.

Have this attitude in yourselves which was also in Christ Jesus, who, as He already existed in the form of God, did not consider equality with God something to be grasped, but emptied Himself by taking the form of a bondservant and being born in the likeness of men. And being found in appearance as a man, He humbled Himself by becoming obedient to the point of death: death on a cross.

questions: How does knowing that Jesus performed miracles through the Holy Spirit (not His divine nature) change your view of what's possible for you?

content.text: Representative Obedience: Every act of obedience that Jesus did as a man he did as us and on our behalf to reconcile us in our disobedience.

Everything that Jesus did in his humanity he did as you. Let me explain. When Jesus was baptized, he was dunked in water as a man, filled with the Holy Spirit as a man and received the affirmation of Father God as a man. He did all this as a human representative for all mankind.

It gets better. Jesus fasted in the wilderness, cast out demons, opened blind eyes, raised Lazarus from the dead, was crucified, buried and resurrected as a mere human being filled with the Holy Spirit. The mystery of the gospel is that he did all of this as us. Jesus didn't just die for us but as us? My God! It gets better.

For we do not have a high priest who is unable to empathize with our weaknesses, but we have one who has been tempted in every way, just as we are—yet he did not sin. Let us then approach God's throne of grace with confidence, so that we may receive mercy and find grace to help us in our time of need.

Jesus, the Son of God, put himself on our level. He reveals himself as a humble servant on a mission to heal the separation of God and man. As a man, he was tempted the same way we all are. Which means we have a God who understands every struggle and mess we find ourselves in. Even though we made ourselves enemies of God in our own minds he came in the likeness of human flesh to receive the disease of sin and in return, he gives us His Spirit as the cure.
He is well acquainted with your pain. There is no human emotion that is off limits with God.

He wants all of you, not just the good parts. You name a place of darkness you have experienced and that is a place that Jesus has been. Not only has Jesus been there but he has defeated that darkness. Jesus makes our union with the Father real. As a good son who followed every word of Papa, He became your sin so you could become a son or daughter.
He made Him who knew no sin to be sin on our behalf, so that we might become the righteousness of God in Him.

questions: How are you different from Jesus in the Hypostatic Union?
How are you the same?

content.text: Let's start with this statement. By design you are exactly just like Jesus. He is the archetype and you are the prototype. In other words Jesus took his own personal blueprint and put it into the creation of every human being. So, what's the difference between you and Jesus? Technically nothing. Jesus has an earthsuit. You have an earthsuit. Jesus received the Holy Spirit in the same way, so do you. So what's the difference?

You may say "Well I'm not God." And I would agree with you on an individualistic level. You alone are not Jesus. But together, aren't we the Body of Jesus? Are we a decapitated body, separated from the head? Absolutely not! We are one with Christ in perfect union. We are seated in heavenly places in Christ at the right hand of the Father. The reality is, as the Body, we have full access to the Trinity through the person of Jesus and His finished work. This is why the Bible says "We have the mind of Christ". Not "I" but "We".
The big difference: Jesus was sinless and his mind was already pure. We however, are sinless because of the blood but our minds still need to be renewed to the reality of Christ.

Each and every one of us are living with some form of delusion that Holy Spirit is healing as we learn to surrender to what is real. This is what Holy Spirit does. He convicts us of righteousness; of who we are in our highest mindset.

If all this is true and you have a full invitation to live in perfect union with God through the Holy Spirit at work within you, who sets the limit to how much like Jesus you can be? Is it your church leaders, society or your family? No, it's you. You set the limit according to your ability to surrender to the reality of Christ. But first we must acknowledge that we could be living an illusion that Jesus never died and resurrected for you to live in. The truth is He rose from the grave so you could experience His reality right here on earth.

I have been crucified with Christ; and it is no longer I who live, but Christ lives in me; and the life which I now live in the flesh I live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and gave Himself up for me.

questions: What holds you back from walking out your faith just like Jesus?
Do you believe that you are a good son or daughter? Why or why not.

sessions.title: Holy Spirit
sessions.synopsis: There is a difference between agreeing with the testimony of the Holy Spirit and being filled with Holy Spirit power. One is a matter of salvation and the other is a requirement to a Christ-like ministry. What part does the Holy Spirit play in our lives? The answer may surprise you.
sessions.verse: Enter into the promises of God. It is your inheritance. You will do more in one year if you are really filled with the Holy Ghost than you could do in fifty years apart from Him.
warmUp: You may or may not have been brought up in church. But either way you have a perception of ideas that you have built up about who Holy Spirit is. Describe the way you see Holy Spirit. What relationship do you have to Spirit and what does He do in your life?

content.text: Often we talk about the Father and the Son but we forget Holy Spirit is the third person of the Trinity. We cannot exclude Holy Spirit from the Perichoresis relationship with The Father and The Son. We must come to the truth that when Holy Spirit does something, God is doing it.

The Spirit of God can feel mysterious at times. Many people are confused by the spooky connotation of an invisible floating energy that does stuff for God. Holy Spirit is not a ghost or an it. Holy Spirit is the Spirit of Christ among us. He is the comforter promised by Jesus, who unites us with the Father and the Son, and transforms us into the image of Christ.
It is for your good that I am going away. Unless I go away, the Advocate will not come to you; but if I go, I will send him to you.
And we see this happen on the Day of Pentecost when his disciples are waiting in the upper room for his promise to be fulfilled.

All Jesus has ever known is intimate communion with the Holy Spirit. He was conceived by the Spirit in the womb of Mary, baptized with the Spirit, and on the cross surrendered His Spirit to fulfill his sacrificial ministry to the Father. The Spirit now ministers this finished work to us.

The Spirit is often described as the comforter who leads us to repentance, The liberator who frees us from the disease of sin and empowers us to live joyfully in service to Christ. By the power of The Spirit we are brought to union with the divine nature.

One of the functions of Holy Spirit that people forget is the conviction of sin. John 16:8 says "When he comes, he will prove the world to be in the wrong about sin and righteousness and judgment." Could it be that Christians have taken Holy Spirit's job to convict people of their sin? This usually comes out in condemnation and judgment with signs that may be true, but hurtful. If we don't allow Holy Spirit to do what Holy Spirit does we could push people farther from knowing God.
If you have said "yes" to the gift of Jesus, then you already have the fullness of His Spirit available to you.

You don't need to fast and plead with God for more of His presence. Holy Spirit does not withhold himself from you. You are as close to God right now in this moment than you will ever be. This reality of constant union is what makes experiencing His tangible manifest presence so easy.

questions: Have you ever experienced or witnessed a manifestation of the Holy Spirit? This could include speaking in tongues, uncontrollable joy, falling out under His power, shaking, open visions, and radical repentance from sin.
How do you feel about these kinds of manifestations of the Spirit? Do you think it's strange? Or does the supernatural excite you?

content.text: How do you feel about the baptism of Holy Spirit? Being completely immersed in the power of God?

It is not for you to know the times or dates the Father has set by his own authority. But you will receive power when the Holy Spirit comes on you; and you will be my witnesses in Jerusalem, and in all Judea and Samaria, and to the ends of the earth.
Holy Spirit Baptism: The enveloping of the Holy Spirit in, around and through a person's life that causes them to function in the power and gifting of God.

Partnership with Holy Spirit is a promise for all believers who are willing to receive. It is necessary to be effective in bringing heaven to earth. The power of Holy Spirit turned Peter from a defeated disciple who was ready to return to fishing, into a bold revivalist who preached the first sermon on the day of Pentecost. 3,000 Jews made a decision to follow Jesus that day.

In Acts 19 Paul arrives in Ephesus and finds some disciples and asks them… "Did you receive the Holy Spirit when you believed?" They answered "No, we have not even heard that there is a Holy Spirit." So Paul asked, "Then what baptism did you receive?" "John's baptism," they replied. Paul said, "John's baptism was a baptism of repentance. He told the people to believe in the one coming after him, that is, in Jesus." On hearing this, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus. When Paul placed his hands on them, the Holy Spirit came on them, and they spoke in tongues and prophesied. There were about twelve men in all.

As for me, I (John) baptize you with water for repentance, but He (Jesus) who is coming after me is mightier than I, and I am not fit to remove His sandals; He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.

In the Gospels, we see Jesus being baptized by John. But we also read that during his baptism, Holy Spirit came and remained with him so He could carry out his mission with power and boldness. Holy Spirit is a requirement for a ministry of power. I would even state that miracles, signs and wonders do not happen apart from Holy Spirit.

questions: Will you allow God to use you to advance His kingdom?
Will you walk in boldness and unapologetically declare his goodness to those around?
Will you pray for the sick and see them healed?
Will you follow the Holy Spirit into dangerous yet adventurous situations that will stir the waters of revival?

content.text: This is what your life is supposed to look like as a Christian and we desperately need Holy Spirit to envelop us. Let's be intentional and allow Holy Spirit to move. Create a space without distraction where you can focus on worshiping our Lord. You can do this with music or without. You can do this alone or with other hungry believers. Just let it be a place where the only agenda is encountering His presence.
Manifestations of the Holy Spirit:

• Speaking in tongues (Heavenly prayer language)
• Prophecy (The ability to speak what God is saying)
• Open Visions (Trances, Vivid dreams, Seeing in the spiritual realm)
• Fire (Literal refining fire moving through your body)
• Laughter (Uncontrollable Joy)
• Shalom (Supernatural peace, clarity and wholeness)
• Healing hands (Fire in your palms, Oil coming from your palms)
• Repentance (Supernatural ability to turn from sin without shame)
• Compassion (The heart of God breaking over the lost and broken)
Ask Holy Spirit for anything right now, what is it? For real anything. Ask big. Ask with boldness. Ask with the expectation of power.
Come, thou Holy Spirit! come, Shine from Thy celestial home Into hearts that make Thee room!

questions: Record your experience. What did you ask for? What did you experience?

sessions.title: Identity Check
sessions.synopsis: How do you test if a vessel has holes in it? You fill it, assess the areas that need repair and patch the broken places. Then you refill it and test it again to make sure the repairs are complete. This process is repeated until wholeness is accomplished. Let's see how far you have come in 6 weeks.
sessions.verse: For as he thinks within himself, so he is.
warmUp: The very first week you participated in an identity exercise that gave you a real assessment of how you perceived God, yourself and How God sees you. That exercise can be done as often as you feel led to. Are you ready to see how far you've come?

content.text: We may ignore, but we can nowhere evade the presence of God.
Remember to answer these questions honestly from your current mindset and not as you would hope to answer in the future.

questions: Take a moment and answer honestly. When I perceive God I see...

content.text: Anyone who has discovered who God has made them to be would never want to be anyone else.

questions: Take a moment and answer honestly. When I perceive myself I see...

content.text: The first two identities are key factors in our awareness of God and self however the third is where the breakthrough happens. This identity is the breath of God that speaks life into us according to His view. The reason this identity is so powerful is because it is not our own perception. It has nothing to do with what we think but everything to do with what God sees and thinks when he looks at us.

questions: Take as long as necessary to listen and write down everything you hear. God when You look at me, what do You see?

content.text: Compare your answers from week one.

questions: How has your perception of who God is shifted?
Are there lies that have been exposed and let go of?
Any differences in your self perception?
Any lies that you still need to let go of?
How has God's perception of you transformed your life over these 6 weeks?

content.text: You can only be like Jesus to the measure of faith that you already are. Identity is not determined by behavior but Identity proceeds behavior.
You wanna be more like Jesus? Well wake up and realize that He has already given you His Spirit, His glory, His likeness and everything you will ever need to be.
